data.title: Baroque Japan Sees FY Net Y1.34B
Baroque Japan FY Loss Y2.58B Vs Net Y945.00M
McKenzie Intelligence Services CEO calls for NOAA dataset paywall amid industry concern at Trump cuts
data.description:
(The Insurer) - Weather data from the U.S. National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA) should go behind a paywall for commercial use, the CEO of geospatial intelligence provider McKenzie Intelligence Services (MIS) told The Insurer.

Since the start of the second Trump administration, NOAA has cut 1,300 employees and is planning another 1,029 layoffs as part of an efficiency drive, Reuters reported on April 1. Some climate scientists have said the quality or timeliness of NOAA's data will suffer as a result of the cuts and the Reinsurance Association of America raised concerns about the sector's reliance on the agency in March.

Speaking to The Insurer at the Insurtech Insights Europe conference last month, MIS CEO Forbes McKenzie discussed the dependence on NOAA data of some vendors that service the insurance sector.

"A lot of companies out there use the NOAA datasets in particular," McKenzie said.

"I'll be really surprised if they're there in 12 months' time, certainly the way the U.S. administration is currently going. I think they'll resurface in three to four years' time, but I think there's going to be significant disruption, which we're already starting to see now from those datasets."

McKenzie said he thought NOAA should charge organisations that make money from its data. "There's an industry based upon free data from the States... I think quite rightly they should be charging for it."

"I'm delighted to pay and I can't say that enough, I think that is appropriate," McKenzie said. "If you're paying for it, you can hold them to account for what they're providing too. So I actually want to pay for it as it happens." He added, "As long as it's free, it runs the risk of not being there."

Lloyd's Lab alumnus MIS provides a portfolio management system that incorporates information on natural and manmade perils from a range of sources. It worked with Lloyd's to provide its services to the whole Lloyd's market between 2021 and 2023 and now works directly with 37 Lloyd's insurers, McKenzie said.

data.title: Italian tycoon emerges as lead investor for CK Hutchison ports, Bloomberg News reports
data.description:
Italian billionaire Gianluigi Aponte's family-run business is emerging as the lead investor of a group seeking to buy 43 ports from conglomerate CK Hutchison , Bloomberg News reported on Monday, citing people familiar with the matter.

The Aponte family's Terminal Investment Ltd (TIL), which manages a diverse portfolio of container terminals according to its website, will be the sole owner of all the ports once the deal is completed, except for two in Panama that would be controlled by BlackRock Inc, Bloomberg reported.

BlackRock's Global Infrastructure Partners will own 51% of the two ports near the Panama Canal, while TIL will own the rest.

CK Hutchison declined to comment.

The port facilities at the strategic waterway account for about 4% of the total value of the deal. CK Hutchison's founder tycoon, Li Ka-shing, is expected to make more than $19 billion in cash, Bloomberg said, citing a person aware of the details.

CK Hutchison has faced an increasing barrage of criticism from China on its decision to sell most of its $22.8 billion port business to BlackRock. The deal has become highly politicised as the conglomerate is thrust into the crosshairs of an escalating China-U.S. trade war.

Shares of CK Hutchison extended gains to 3.1% after the report, versus a 2.1% rise in the main Hang Seng Index

data.title: Spanish stocks - Factors to watch on April 14
data.description:
The following Spanish stocks may be affected by newspaper reports and other factors on Friday. Reuters has not verified the newspaper reports, and cannot vouch for their accuracy:

TELEFONICA

Spanish telecom company Telefonica said on Sunday it had agreed to sell its Peruvian unit, Telefonica del Peru , which recently filed for bankruptcy protection, to Argentina's Integra Tec International for about 900,000 euros ($1.02 million).

OHLA

Spanish construction firm OHLA was chosen by ViasChile, owned by Abertis and Abu Dhabi Investments Authority, to build two tunnels near Santiago de Chile for about 300 million euros ($341.10 million), newspaper El Economista said on Monday, citing unidentified sources.

AENA

Low-cost airline Ryanair expects to withdraw more flights from medium and small Spanish airports next winter and in 2026, if Spanish airport operator Aena does not lower the fees to operate from these, the airline's senior executive Eddie Wilson told newspaper El Economista in an interview published on Monday.

IBERDROLA

Spanish utility company Iberdrola said on Friday its first quarter net electricity production was down 13.3% year-on-year. The company plans to sell its cogeneration business by selling seven manure-based plants in Spain with a total capacity of 50 MegaWatts, El Economista reported on Monday, citing unnamed sources.

INMOBILIARIA COLONIAL

Colonial started a buyback programme for up to 25 million euros worth of shares, the company said on Friday.

Convatec Upgrades Sales Outlook After U.S. Delays Medicare Coverage Decisions
data.description:
(06:04 GMT) *Convatec Sees InnovaMatrix Sales of $75M in FY25
(06:04 GMT) *Convatec Had Seen InnovaMatrix Rev of $50M in FY25
(06:25 GMT) Convatec Upgrades Sales Outlook After U.S. Delays Medicare Coverage Decisions

By Michael Susin

Convatec upgraded the sales outlook for its placental medical device in the U.S. due to delays in Medicare coverage decisions.

The company said Monday that it now expects sales of its InnovaMatrix product to be around $75 million in 2025 compared with the previous target of around $50 million.

The upgrade follows the postponement by the Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services agency of decisions on whether certain products for the treatment of diabetic foot ulcers and venous leg ulcers would be covered by Medicare.

The company said U.S. patients will continue to have access to its device. There are no other changes to its financial guidance, Convatec said.

Write to Michael Susin at michael.susin@wsj.com

data.title: Market Chatter: Fortum Mulls Buying Uniper Assets in Sweden
data.description:
Fortum is considering the acquisition of its former subsidiary Uniper's highly profitable assets in Sweden, London's Financial Times reported Sunday, citing two people familiar with the discussions.

Germany-based Uniper has interests in three active nuclear power plants and owns 74 hydro power plants in Sweden.

In a statement to the publication, Fortum said it secured the right of first offer on the Swedish assets as part of its deal three years ago to let go of all its shareholding in Uniper. The Finnish energy company maintained that it would be interested in the assets if they became available.

Sources told FT Swedish state-owned power company Vattenfall is also a possible bidder for Uniper's Swedish assets, while a competing offer for a portion or all of Uniper from Norwegian energy company Equinor is also a possibility. The people added that the assets could also pique the interest of Czech billionaire Daniel Křetínský.

Fortum and Uniper did not immediately respond to requests for comments from MT Newswires.

Disclaimer

Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.

data.title: Rhythm Biosciences Receives Quality Certification from UK Standards Body
data.description:
Rhythm Biosciences said that it has retained its ISO 13485:2016 certification for the seventh year from the UK's British Standards Institution (BSI), according to a Monday filing with the Australian bourse.

This year's certification also includes an expanded scope, now covering the design, manufacturing, distribution, installation, and servicing of in-vitro medical devices, the filing said.

data.title: Tadco Extends Hydroponic Greenhouse Project Deal with Neom's Topian in Saudi Arabia
data.description:
Tabuk Agricultural Development (SASE:6040), d/b/a Tadco, said Monday it extended by six months the memorandum of understanding signed with Neom food company Topian to develop and operate a hydroponic greenhouse for fruits and vegetables.

The agreement's extension, effective Thursday, will allow the Saudi Arabia-based agricultural company and Topian to conduct further feasibility work and produce necessary reports, including environmental and social impact assessments and infrastructure.

data.title: UK's Distil to relaunch vodka brand in US
data.description:
RedLeg Spiced Rum maker Distil said on Monday it was relaunching its Blavod Black Vodka in the U.S., adding that the current tariff levels were manageable, and if they were lifted in the future, it would positively impact their supply chain.

Distil, which owns premium drinks brands, is partnering with AIKO Importers to reintroduce the Blavod Black vodka brand, years after its previous distributor withdrew during the COVID-19 pandemic.

data.title: UK's Energean inks new gas supply contract in Israel
data.description: UK-based gas producer Energean said on Monday that its Israeli subsidiary has signed a new 17-year gas sale and purchase contract with Kesem Energy to supply fuel for a new power plant in Israel.

data.title: REG - EPE Special Opps Ltd EPE Special Opp-EO.P EPE Special Opp-EL.P - Transaction in Own Shares
data.description:
RNS Number : 8618E EPE Special Opportunities Limited 14 April 2025  

EPE Special Opportunities Limited

Transaction in Own Shares

EPE Special Opportunities Limited ("ESO" or the "Company") announces that, on 11 April 2025, it purchased 30,000 of the Company's shares of 5 pence each ("Ordinary Shares") at a weighted average price of 140.00 pence per share.

The purchased Ordinary Shares will be held by the Company in treasury.

The number of Ordinary Shares in issue, not held by the Company in treasury, following the transaction and the figure to be used as the denominator for calculations of interests in the Company's voting rights is 28,982,847 Ordinary Shares. The number of Ordinary Shares held by the Company in treasury following the transaction is 5,633,707 Ordinary Shares.

In accordance with Article 5(1)(b) of Regulation (EU) No 596/2014 (the Market Abuse Regulation), the schedule below contains detailed information about the purchases of Ordinary Shares made by Numis Securities Limited ("Deutsche Numis") on behalf of the Company.

This announcement does not constitute, or form part of, an offer or any solicitation of an offer for securities in any jurisdiction.

The person responsible for releasing this information on behalf of the Company is Amanda Robinson of Langham Hall Fund Management (Jersey) Limited.
